title: Thelypteris nymphalis
intro: Thelypteris nymphalis, also known as Nephrolepis nymphalis or maidenhair fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris nymphalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris nymphalis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
taxonomy: Thelypteris nymphalis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This family is a group of ferns commonly known as maiden ferns or autumn ferns. The genus Thelypteris is a group of species of deciduous and evergreen ferns, native to Asia and the Americas. Thelypteris nymphalis belongs to the order Polypodiales, which includes a large and diverse group of ferns. The exact classification of Thelypteris nymphalis may also be expressed using the binomial nomenclature system.
